headline: Beans hit 55 days — pick before afternoon storms.
weather_notes: Run zones before noon today — 3 days dry at 93°F exceeds the 1-day heat threshold; skip only if today's 60% afternoon storms actually deliver rain.
Harvest beans (c2/c3/c4) and check blackberries before noon — today's 60% afternoon storm window will knock ripe pods and bruise cane fruit.
Hold all foliar feeds and sprays until Thursday morning — today's 60% afternoon storms will wash off applications before they absorb.
Plan a zone run for Thursday morning — 95-99°F with no rain forecast Thursday-Friday will push soil past the re-water threshold within a day of today's storms clearing.

surfaced_tasks.title: Tip new blackberry primocanes if 3–4 ft tall
surfaced_tasks.reason: 12 days overdue; this morning before today's 60% afternoon storms is the clearest outdoor window of the week.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest c2/c3/c4 beans before noon storms
suggested_tasks.description: Jade Bush (c3/c4) and Red Swan (c2) hit 55 days today — pick this morning before today's 60% afternoon storms knock ripe pods.

suggested_tasks.title: Feed w1 strawberries with berry food
suggested_tasks.description: 49 days since the Jun 3 berry food application — apply Friday morning after today's storms clear and soil surface dries slightly.
